assertions.description: The WebSocket constructor is exposed; LNA restrictions gate connections from this constructor, they do not remove it.
The LNA permission is restricted to secure contexts; the page must be HTTPS or localhost.
navigator.permissions is exposed so userland can probe the local-network / loopback-network permissions.
WebSocket exposes the CONNECTING/OPEN/CLOSING/CLOSED constants so userland can observe the connection state including LNA-gated failures.
WebSocket dispatches a CloseEvent when a connection is denied (e.g. by LNA permission); CloseEvent is exposed.
Constructing a WebSocket with a loopback URL does not synchronously throw; LNA gating happens during the handshake, not at constructor time.
